description: We’re the government’s adviser for the natural environment in England. We help to protect and restore our natural world. Natural England is an executive non-departmental public body, sponsored by the Department for Environment, Food & Rural Affairs .

details.body:
We’re the government’s adviser for the natural environment in England. We help to protect and restore our natural world.

Natural England is an executive non-departmental public body, sponsored by the Department for Environment, Food & Rural Affairs.

image.alt_text: Photograph of green-winged orchids at Mendip NNR

ordered_featured_documents.summary: April to early June is the prime window to experience the reserve’s spectacular wildflowers and wildlife
ordered_featured_documents.title: Spring into the Mendip National Nature Reserve

image.alt_text: A photograph of the research vessel Cefas Endeavour.

ordered_featured_documents.summary: A four‑week seabed mapping survey will bring together scientists from 11 organisations to improve understanding of the marine environment.
ordered_featured_documents.title: UK scientists unite for seabed survey off the south‑west coast

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Five species ranging from turtle doves to rare diamond-backed spider will receive a major funding boost
ordered_featured_documents.title: Threatened species set for major funding boost

image.alt_text: Photograph of the blue-based Earthtongue (Microglossum cyanobasis)

ordered_featured_documents.summary: UK first for Sussex nature reserve – member of public stumbles on rare find.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Fungus among us!

image.alt_text: A signpost showing the new nation al trail

ordered_featured_documents.summary: National Trail opening is culmination years of work by Natural England and partners alongside £5.5 million government investment for upgrade
ordered_featured_documents.title: “Wainwright way” becomes England’s newest National Trail

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Part of the King’s Series, establishing 25 new National Nature Reserves by 2028. Surrey’s National Nature Reserve home to rare ground-nesting birds this spring
ordered_featured_documents.title: Spring awakens at Wealden Heaths National Nature Reserve

ordered_featured_links.title: How to access Natural England's maps and data

details.secondary_corporate_information_pages: Read about the types of information we routinely publish in our Publication scheme. Our Personal information charter explains how we treat your personal information. Read our policy on Social media use. Find out About our services.

role_appointments.title: Dr Tony Juniper CBE - Ex officio Defra board member

details.body: The Chair of Natural England is responsible for leading the organisation and making sure that it fulfills all of its goals.
The Interim Chief Executive, Natural England is responsible for the day to day management of the organisation.
The Chief Executive, Natural England is responsible for the day to day management of the organisation.

Non-executive board members of Natural England have collective responsibility for the strategic direction and overall performance of the organisation. They make sure that Natural England is properly and effectively managed and provide stewardship for the public funds entrusted to the organisation.

The Chief Officer, Strategy is responsible for:

  • developing and implementing Natural England’s strategy through partnership working 
  • leading our portfolios to deliver our strategy; including bolstering the resilience of our landscapes and seas, driving sustainable development, better connecting people with nature, and supporting greener farming and fisheries
  • providing government advice on policy and legislation
  • developing and piloting new delivery mechanisms

The COO is responsible for leadership of the:

  • 14 area teams that deliver Natural England’s outcomes across the breadth of its remit
  • national teams that support outcome delivery and enable innovation and strategic change

Responsibilities include:

  • bringing alive Defra’s strategy and joint working across the Defra Group at a local level
  • managing Natural England’s reputation and profile at a local level
  • gathering intelligence to inform policy development and strategy development
  • developing better ways of doing things
  • delivering cultural change associated with organisational reforms

The Chief Officer, Strategy is responsible for:

  • developing and implementing Natural England’s strategy through partnership working 
  • leading our portfolios to deliver our strategy; including bolstering the resilience of our landscapes and seas, driving sustainable development, better connecting people with nature, and supporting greener farming and fisheries
  • providing government advice on policy and legislation
  • developing and piloting new delivery mechanisms

The COO is responsible for leadership of the:

  • 14 area teams that deliver Natural England’s outcomes across the breadth of its remit
  • national teams that support outcome delivery and enable innovation and strategic change

Responsibilities include:

  • bringing alive Defra’s strategy and joint working across the Defra Group at a local level
  • managing Natural England’s reputation and profile at a local level
  • gathering intelligence to inform policy development and strategy development
  • developing better ways of doing things
  • delivering cultural change associated with organisational reforms

The Chief Strategy and Reform Officer is responsible for developing and maintaining a single integrated organisational view of the outcomes Natural England is seeking to achieve and how it will best realise these ambitions through Conservation 21. Responsibilities include:

  • leading the organisation’s strategy and direction by developing innovative and strategic solutions to the environment’s and the organisation’s conservation issues
  • leading and embedding change, innovation and reform across the organisation
  • providing advice to government to inform strategy and policy development across the organisation’s remit
  • developing broader cross-government opportunities to benefit people and the natural environment, for example through strategic infrastructure planning
  • building our reputation and sustainable positive relationships with national partners and stakeholders

role_appointments.title: Alan Law - Chief Strategy and Reform Officer

details.body:
CSD has a budget of £55 million and 500 staff. These staff provide a capability to generate, manage and share scientific, technical and analytical information on nature, landscape and access. They have a wide range of scientific, analytical and technical expertise, and include:

  • marine, terrestrial and freshwater ecologists
  • geologists
  • economists
  • social scientists
  • data analysts
  • statisticians

The Legal, Governance and External Affairs Officer is responsible for:

  • all legal services
  • providing guidance on strategically important casework to the Board and Executive
  • corporate governance and assurance
  • information management and data security

role.title: Chief Legal, Governance and External Affairs Officer

role_appointments.title: Navroza Ladha - Chief Legal and Governance Officer

details.body:
The Chief Officer, Business Management is responsible for:

  • cross-organisation business planning
  • management information and performance reporting
  • organisational transformation programme
  • development of change initiatives and delivery of people strategy

role_appointments.title: Kirsty Carter-Brown - Chief Officer Business Management and Change

details.description:
Contact the Wildlife Incident Investigation Scheme (WIIS) if you suspect wildlife or companion animals have been poisoned by professional pesticide use.

Do not report domestic incidents of poisoning to WIIS. Report domestic incidents to the police, RSPCA or your local council.

Seek immediate veterinary treatment if an animal or bird is suffering.

The WIIS voicemail is monitored from Monday to Friday, 9am to 4:30pm. It is not monitored during the weekend or public holidays.

details.body:
CSD has a budget of £55 million and 500 staff. These staff provide a capability to generate, manage and share scientific, technical and analytical information on nature, landscape and access. They have a wide range of scientific, analytical and technical expertise, and include:

  • marine, terrestrial and freshwater ecologists
  • geologists
  • economists
  • social scientists
  • data analysts
  • statisticians
The Chief Executive, Natural England is responsible for the day to day management of the organisation.

The COO is responsible for leadership of the:

  • 14 area teams that deliver Natural England’s outcomes across the breadth of its remit
  • national teams that support outcome delivery and enable innovation and strategic change

Responsibilities include:

  • bringing alive Defra’s strategy and joint working across the Defra Group at a local level
  • managing Natural England’s reputation and profile at a local level
  • gathering intelligence to inform policy development and strategy development
  • developing better ways of doing things
  • delivering cultural change associated with organisational reforms

The Chief Strategy and Reform Officer is responsible for developing and maintaining a single integrated organisational view of the outcomes Natural England is seeking to achieve and how it will best realise these ambitions through Conservation 21. Responsibilities include:

  • leading the organisation’s strategy and direction by developing innovative and strategic solutions to the environment’s and the organisation’s conservation issues
  • leading and embedding change, innovation and reform across the organisation
  • providing advice to government to inform strategy and policy development across the organisation’s remit
  • developing broader cross-government opportunities to benefit people and the natural environment, for example through strategic infrastructure planning
  • building our reputation and sustainable positive relationships with national partners and stakeholders

The Chief Transformation Officer is responsible for delivering the cultural changes needed for Natural England 2020 change programme.

Responsibilities include:

  • ensuring resources and priority allocated to delivery of the organisational and other changes
  • lead, align and deliver the overall programme managing risks and dependencies
  • aligning NE change activity with the overall Defra activity and Target Operating Model
  • leading on engagement and communicating with all staff
  • operating as a member of the Natural England Senior leadership team
The Chairman of Natural England is responsible for leading the organisation and making sure that it fulfills all of its goals.
